subject: Biology
title: The Endoplasmic Reticulum
description: A 60-second lesson on The Endoplasmic Reticulum.

hook.text: A maze of folded membranes fills much of the cell. This is the endoplasmic reticulum.

buildup.text: Rough ER is studded with ribosomes and builds proteins. Smooth ER makes lipids.

discovery.text: Liver cells have massive smooth ER networks because they process toxins like alcohol.

twist.text: Misfolded proteins in the ER trigger stress responses. If unchecked, the cell self-destructs.

climax.text: The ER is the factory floor—raw molecules enter, finished products leave.

punchline.text: A hidden factory in every cell you own.

quiz.question: What does rough endoplasmic reticulum do?
